conventions.rule: Every Ruby file starts with `# frozen_string_literal: true` (excluded only for db/migrate/** and spec/dummy/**)
Use double-quoted strings; trailing commas in multiline literals/args/hashes; line length 120
STI field subclasses live one-per-file under app/models/typed_eav/field/ (do not reintroduce a single types.rb)
Boolean/predicate methods end in `?` (e.g. array_field?, optionable?, unscoped?)
Postgres index names use `idx_te_*` prefix to fit the 63-byte identifier limit
AR table names are namespaced + snake_case + plural: typed_eav_fields, typed_eav_values, typed_eav_options, typed_eav_sections
Inline rubocop disables must include a justification comment on the disable line; pair with a matching enable
Comments are rationale-first (`why`, not `what`) and often phrased as `Without this, ...`; tied to specific commits where helpful
User-facing exceptions list available options and tell the caller how to fix it (e.g. ArgumentError messages with `Available fields: ...`)
Internal sentinels declared `private_constant`; documented public sentinels (UNSET_SCOPE, ALL_SCOPES) intentionally are not
RSpec uses opt-in `:unscoped` / `:scoping` example metadata; default is no wrapping (earlier blanket wrapping masked scope-collision bugs)
Registry is NOT reset between specs — preserves `has_typed_eav` class-load registrations so registration tests stay meaningful
Regression specs live in spec/regressions/; pending tests describe desired (not-yet-fixed) behavior and auto-un-pend when fix lands
Conventional Commits: `<type>(<scope>): <subject>` with present-tense imperative; `!` marks breaking changes
Generators ship with explicit Thor `namespace` declarations (fix per commit ccb04b3); fail-closed admin auth hook returns `head :not_found`
